rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are visible and properly declared in the same annex or imported packages.
Resolve type mismatches in AGREE expressions by aligning declared types with actual expression types (int, real, bool).
Remove invalid guarantee or assume clauses that reference undefined or improperly typed identifiers.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, always ensure that named things are valid expressions with consistent types.
Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE annexes to avoid resolution errors.
Validate that all numeric and Boolean expressions in AGREE match the expected scalar types (int, real, bool).
When generating AGREE contracts, prefer explicit type declarations and avoid ambiguous or error-prone expressions.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE requires consistent types in binary expressions; e.g., real comparisons must involve real operands.
AGREE const and eq declarations must use valid scalar types: int, real, bool.
AGREE assume/guarantee clauses must reference identifiers that are either visible features or locally declared helpers.
anti_patterns: Do not add new architecture elements to resolve AGREE diagnostics; instead, correct or remove invalid clauses.
Avoid using subcomponent ports or implementation-specific paths in component type contracts.
Do not assume that AADL identifiers are automatically available in AGREE; they must be explicitly declared or visible.
irrelevant_topics: Component type vs implementation scope boundaries
